<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Evaluating deployment options**

I'm noting deployment architecture choices for Next.js, Fastify, and PostgreSQL services. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s a concise picture that matches your repo: **Next.js 15** in `apps/web`, a **Fastify** API in `services/api` that talks to **PostgreSQL** via `DATABASE_URL` (`pg`), plus **ingest** and **ai** services.

### Database

Use **managed PostgreSQL** and point both API and any other services at the same connection string:

- **[Neon](https://neon.tech)** — serverless Postgres, good with serverless/edge-style frontends and variable API traffic.  
- **[Supabase](https://supabase.com)** — Postgres + extras (auth, storage) if you want that later.  
- **[Railway](https://railway.app) / [Render](https://render.com)** — simple Postgres + app hosting in one place.  
- **AWS RDS / Google Cloud SQL / Azure** — if you’re already on a cloud account and want full control.

Any of these work as long as you set `DATABASE_URL` (and run migrations/seed however you already do locally).

### Website + API (typical split)

- **Frontend:** **[Vercel](https://vercel.com)** for `apps/web` is the usual choice for Next.js (build `apps/web`, set env vars for public API URL if the browser calls it).  
- **API:** Deploy `services/api` as a **long-running Node** service on **Railway, Render, Fly.io**, or a small **VM/container** (ECS, Cloud Run, etc.). Set `DATABASE_URL` there; allow the DB’s network rules to accept that host.

That’s the pattern: **Vercel (web) + managed Postgres + one Node host (API)**.

### If you want fewer moving parts

**Railway** or **Render** can host **Postgres + Next.js + Fastify** in one project (multiple services), which is easier operationally at the cost of less “Next-optimized” hosting than Vercel.

### Ingest / AI workers

Treat `services/ingest` and `services/ai` as **background jobs**: same repo, separate deployables with **cron** (Render/Railway scheduled jobs, GitHub Actions + HTTP trigger, Cloud Scheduler, etc.) and the same `DATABASE_URL` (and any API keys they need).

---

**Practical default:** **Neon (or Supabase) for Postgres**, **Vercel for the Next app**, **Railway or Render for the Fastify API** (and optional cron for ingest/ai). If you tell me your budget (free tier vs paid) and whether the API must be private to the frontend only, we can narrow to one stack and env var layout.
